name: WESTINGHOUSE AIR BRAKE TECHNOLOGIES CORP
business: Wabtec is a global provider of value-added, technology-based locomotives, equipment, systems and services for the freight rail and passenger transit industries, and the mining, marine, and industrial markets and applications. Our highly engineered rail and transit products, which are intended to enhance safety, improve productivity and reduce maintenance costs for customers, can be found on most locomotives, freight cars, passenger transit cars and buses around the world.
sellsTo: manufacturers of original equipment such as locomotives and freight cars
companies in the mining, marine, and industrial markets
manufacturers of passenger transit vehicles and buses
companies in the electrical generation, distribution, and charging industries

exposures.sentence: Transport intelligence such as Industrial/mobile Internet of Things ("IoT") hardware & software, edge-to-cloud, on and off-board analytics and rules, and asset performance management
provides nondestructive testing, remote visual inspection and analytical instruments solutions for mission critical assets

customers include large, publicly traded railroads, leasing companies, manufacturers of original equipment such as locomotives and freight cars, utilities, and companies in the mining, marine, and industrial markets
Positive Train Control ("PTC") equipment and electronically controlled pneumatic braking products
Doors, window assemblies, accessibility lifts, ramps and electric charging solutions for buses
Railway electronics, including event recorders, monitoring equipment and end of train devices
We are conducting collaborative research and development efforts with the National Laboratories to support the use of hydrogen to lower emissions across the rail industry.
The advancement of artificial intelligence technologies may significantly accelerate the pace and broaden the scope of technological innovation impacting the industry.
We face cyber-security and data protection risks relating to cyber-attacks and information technology failures that could cause loss of confidential information and other business disruptions.

catalysts.sentence: On July 1, 2025, the Company acquired Inspection Technologies for approximately $1.788 billion.
On December 1, 2025, the Company acquired Frauscher, a global market leader in train detection, wayside object control solutions and axle counting systems, for approximately $792 million.
In February 2025, Wabtec announced Integration 3.0, a three-year strategic initiative to target incremental run rate synergies currently estimated to be between $115 million to $140 million by 2028.

catalysts.event: Signed $2.2 billion in new locomotive orders in North America
catalysts.sentence: During the fourth quarter of 2025, Wabtec signed $2.2 billion in new locomotive orders in North America, which included $1.3 billion for locomotive modernizations and $0.9 billion for new locomotives.

catalysts.event: Announced agreement with KTZ for locomotives and service support
catalysts.sentence: In the third quarter of 2025, Wabtec announced an agreement with National Company Kazakhstan Temir Zholy ("KTZ"), the national railway of Kazakhstan, to deliver Evolution Series locomotives and provide long-term service support.

catalysts.event: Announced definitive agreement to acquire Dellner Couplers
catalysts.sentence: On March 18, 2025, Wabtec announced a definitive agreement to acquire Dellner Couplers, a global leader in highly engineered safety-critical train connection systems and services for passenger rail rolling stock, for approximately 890 million.

tamClaims.claim: The global rail market is forecast to grow at a compound annual growth rate of 3% through 2027.
tamClaims.sentence: The 2024 biennial edition of the study concluded that the global rail market grew 2.7% in the 2021 to 2023 period as the industry started recovering from the negative impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic and forecasts the industry to continue with a compound annual growth rate of 3% through 2027.
